result: I don’t have personal wishes, but based on the evidence, one change with the biggest ripple effects would be to eliminate harmful air pollution from burning fuels—outdoors and indoors—by switching to clean energy and clean cooking. It would prevent millions of premature deaths each year, improve child development and productivity, and sharply cut greenhouse-gas emissions. Cleaner air also reduces healthcare costs, boosts crop yields, and narrows inequities, especially for women and children most exposed to indoor smoke. One lever, many wins.

content.text: I don’t have personal wishes, but based on the evidence, one change with the biggest ripple effects would be to eliminate harmful air pollution from burning fuels—outdoors and indoors—by switching to clean energy and clean cooking. It would prevent millions of premature deaths each year, improve child development and productivity, and sharply cut greenhouse-gas emissions. Cleaner air also reduces healthcare costs, boosts crop yields, and narrows inequities, especially for women and children most exposed to indoor smoke. One lever, many wins.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
